SnowDistribution: Firm snow - ice on most aspects.  Good visibility.   Winds 20 -30 mph progress difficult at times with stronger gusts
Comment: Despite a rising freezing level there was still a high wind chill on the hills. Very icy underfoot.
WeatherInfluences: The freezing level will rise above the summits with light rain at all levels during the day. Strong Southerly winds throughout.
SnowStability: Surface layers of the snow will soften but the snowpack will remain generally well bonded and stable. Greatest cover will be mainly on plateau areas and South-West through North-West to North aspects above 850 metres. The avalanche hazard will be Low
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A dry and sunny day. A cold start with the freezing level rising throughout, around +2 degrees at 900 metres by early afternoon. Winds were Westerlies for most to the day, initially quite strong but easing later.
ObservedSnowStability: The snow cover is firm and often icy, only softening where exposed to the sun but remains well bonded and stable throughout. General cover is generally good above 900 metres, particularly on South-West through North-West to North aspects and high plateau\'s. Becoming increasingly patchy below this level. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Firm snow - snow ice on most aspects. Cloudy on the tops at first clearing later. Relatively calm conditions in the afternoon after a blustery start.
Comment: The snow surface will remain very firm and consequential in the event of an uncontrolled slide. With a complex inversion expected, be alert to the snow surface remaining firm above 600 metres, in spite of drizzle and a gradually rising freezing level.
WeatherInfluences: A largely dry day with drizzle at all levels. Possibility of sleet briefly reaching summit levels of 1200 metres. Winds with be Southerly throughout the period increasing from light to gale force. A settled airmass will result in a temperature inversion with icy ground surfaces from 600 metres upwards overnight. The freezing level will be above the summits during day light hours.
SnowStability: The snowpack will be very firm and stable on all aspects and elevations. The greatest snow cover will remain in high coires and plateau areas above 800 metres, with snow patches and ribbons extending to lower elevations. The snow surface is expected to be very firm and icy, despite a rising freezing level. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A trace of snow was evident in the morning above 600 metres. Winds were gale force South-South-Westerly overnight, with moderate to light Westerlies during the day.  The freezing level was around 1000 metres at midday.
ObservedSnowStability: Isolated and avoidable deposits of fresh snow were present on North-West to North-East slopes above 950 metres, although these are not considered significant. The snow pack is firm and icy on all aspects and altitudes, softening only below 800 metres in wind sheltered areas were subject to solar radiation. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Sparse snow cover mostly bare ground patchy in places. Good visibility.   Winds 20 -30 mph progress difficult at times with stronger gusts.
Comment: A mild day in prospect. Old snowpack will remain firm and icy for most of the day.
WeatherInfluences: Winds will remain strong to Gale force generally Southerly. Freezing level around 600m overnight will rise to around 1300m in the day. Some showers are expected during the morning falling as sleet or rain at summit levels then dry during the afternoon.
SnowStability: The patchy and limited snowpack that remains mostly above 750 metres on North-West through North to Easterly aspects will remain very firm and stable throughout, although softening slightly later in the afternoon as it warms.. The dusting of new snow on the tops will linger on Northerly aspects but will mostly disappear during the day as temperatures rise. The avalanche hazard will be Low
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Overnight showers had left a dusting of snow above 800 metres or so by morning, this has slowly disappeared but still lingers in Northerly aspects. A dry day with strong Southerly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: By morning some thin insignificant snow lay on most aspects above 600 metres or so. Gradually disappearing as the day progressed but lingering in shady Northerly aspects. The old snowpack that now remains is well bonded and stable throughout, cover is mostly patchy but significant areas of hard snow is present in some gullies, corrie backwalls and summit slopes, mostly above 750 metres. The avalanche hazard is Low

SnowDistribution: Very firm snow in most locations.  Clear conditions with some sunshine.  Winds 20 -30 mph initially but decreased through the day.
Comment: Despite the rising temperatures and rain the snow surface is likely to be reasonably firm with potential for serious consequences in the event of a trip or slip.
WeatherInfluences: After a dry night rain will develop at all levels during the day. The freezing level will rise above all summits by dawn with Southerly winds increasing in strength to become strong during daylight hours.
SnowStability: The snowpack will remain well bonded, firm and stable.  Largest areas will remain on North-West through North to East aspects above 900 metres, snow cover will be more patchy elsewhere. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A dry day with some sunshine and moderate mainly westerly winds. The freezing level rose to around 1000 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is well bonded, very firm and stable. Largest areas remain on North-West through North to East aspects above around 900 metres. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Firm snow - ice on most aspects. Good visibility. Winds approx 10 - 20 mph fresh, cooling.
Comment: Although the snow will start to slowly soften throughout daylight hours, there is still potential for a slip or fall to have serious consequences. Possibility of rock and ice fall from the cliffs during Wednesday daytime.
WeatherInfluences: Southerly winds will increase through Tuesday night to become very strong. Light rain is expected during the day with the freezing level remaining well above the summits.
SnowStability: The snowpack will have good stability throughout. It will remain firm and icy overnight, then slowly start to soften during the daytime. Snow cover is now mostly confined to North-West through North to Easterly aspects above 850 metres, with only patches remaining below this level, which will continue to thaw throughout Wednesday. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A dry day with the freezing level rising steadily to above the summits. Moderate Westerly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: A trace of new snow fell overnight down to 650 metres, much of this disappearing through the daytime. The snowpack is firm, icy and stable in all locations. Snow cover is mainly confined to North-West through North to Easterly aspects above 850 metres, with only patches remaining below this level. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Very firm snow. Good visibility. Winds 10 - 20 mph.
Comment: The snowpack will be very firm overnight and in the morning with only a little softening expected during the day. Cornice collapse a possibility in daytime rain.
WeatherInfluences: South-South-Easterly winds increase overnight to become very strong during the day. After a dry night precipitation during the day will fall as snow above 1250 metres. The freezing level will rise to above the summits around dawn.
SnowStability: The snowpack will remain well bonded and stable. Fresh snow accumulations on the highest summits are expected to be sleet or wet snow and have good stability, deposits are not expected to be significant. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: It was a dry day with moderate Westerly winds. After a cold night the freezing level rose to around 1000 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack has refrozen and is firm and stable in all locations. The avalanche hazard is Low.
